---
title: "Complete DGP"
subtitle: "The full causal graph behind every generated run, from the scripted macro layer to the paperwork an analyst receives"
mermaid:
  theme: neutral
---

Every mechanism on the [Theory](theory.qmd) page and every hidden table on
[Checking ground truth](ground-truth.qmd) fits into one causal graph. This
page draws it whole: the macro layer that never listens to anything (top),
the micro decisions recomputed every period (middle), the always-conserved
true state those decisions produce, and the recording layer's one-way tap
that turns true state into the paperwork an analyst actually receives
(bottom).

::: {#dag-figure}
```{mermaid}
graph TD
    subgraph MACRO[Macro layer: scripted, exogenous]
        CAL[Calendar: day-of-week, holidays]
        WX[Weather: temperature, rain]
        SHOCK[Macro cost shocks: Poisson events]
        TAX[Tax schedule: VAT, profit tax]
        POP[Household population: arrivals and departures]
    end

    subgraph DERIVED[Derived macro processes]
        DEMMOD[Demand modifier M_ct]
        TRAFFIC[Traffic modifier Lambda_t]
        COSTMULT[Wholesale CostMult_ct]
        BUDGET[Household budget B_iw]
    end

    CAL --> DEMMOD
    WX --> DEMMOD
    CAL --> TRAFFIC
    WX --> TRAFFIC
    SHOCK --> COSTMULT
    SHOCK --> BUDGET

    subgraph OPENING[Owner opening decision, one-shot]
        TRUEDEM[True demand D_cl]
        BELIEF["Owner belief D_hat_cl = D_cl times e to the delta_c"]
        MILP[Opening MILP: location, assortment, quantities, staffing]
    end

    POP --> TRUEDEM
    TRUEDEM --> BELIEF
    BELIEF --> MILP

    subgraph MICRO[Micro layer: recomputed every period]
        PANTRY[Customer pantry P_ict]
        NEED[Category need theta_ict]
        UTIL[Purchase utility U_is]
        CHOICE[Purchase choice: softmax]
        PRICE[Shelf price: menu-cost smoothing]
        RESTOCK[Adaptive restocking: trailing 4-week average]
        SPOIL[Spoilage: Binomial]
        EXPAND[Expansion decision: retained-earnings threshold]
    end

    DEMMOD --> NEED
    PANTRY --> NEED
    NEED --> UTIL
    UTIL --> CHOICE
    PRICE --> UTIL
    BUDGET --> CHOICE
    TRAFFIC --> CHOICE
    CHOICE --> PANTRY

    COSTMULT --> PRICE
    TAX --> PRICE

    CHOICE --> RESTOCK
    MILP --> RESTOCK
    RESTOCK --> SPOIL
    WX --> SPOIL
    SHOCK --> SPOIL

    RESTOCK --> EXPAND
    CHOICE --> EXPAND

    subgraph TRUTH[True simulated state, always exactly conserved]
        TRUESTOCK[True stock S_st]
        TRUELEDGER[True ledger pi_realized]
    end

    CHOICE --> TRUESTOCK
    RESTOCK --> TRUESTOCK
    SPOIL --> TRUESTOCK
    CHOICE --> TRUELEDGER
    RESTOCK --> TRUELEDGER
    TAX --> TRUELEDGER
    EXPAND --> TRUELEDGER

    subgraph RECORDING[Recording layer, applied only at export]
        DEFECTS[Paperwork defects D_st]
    end

    subgraph VISIBLE[visible folder: what an analyst receives]
        RECEIPTS[receipts.csv]
        PROCURE[procurement.csv]
        INVEOD[inventory_eod.csv]
        WRITEOFFS[write_offs.csv]
        LEDGER[cost_sheet.csv]
    end

    TRUESTOCK --> DEFECTS
    TRUELEDGER --> DEFECTS
    DEFECTS --> RECEIPTS
    DEFECTS --> PROCURE
    DEFECTS --> INVEOD
    SPOIL --> WRITEOFFS
    TRUELEDGER --> LEDGER

    subgraph HIDDEN[hidden folder: the answer key]
        IMPERF[hidden_imperfections]
        HIDDEMAND[hidden_hidden_demand]
    end

    DEFECTS --> IMPERF
    CHOICE --> HIDDEMAND
```
:::

<div class="dag-zoom-row">
<button id="dag-zoom-btn" class="dag-zoom-btn" type="button">Zoom in</button>
</div>

<div id="dag-modal" class="dag-modal" aria-hidden="true">
<div class="dag-modal-toolbar">
<button id="dag-zoom-in" class="dag-modal-btn" type="button" title="Zoom in">+</button>
<button id="dag-zoom-out" class="dag-modal-btn" type="button" title="Zoom out">&minus;</button>
<button id="dag-zoom-fit" class="dag-modal-btn" type="button" title="Fit to screen">Fit</button>
<button id="dag-modal-close" class="dag-modal-btn dag-modal-close" type="button" title="Close">Close</button>
</div>
<div id="dag-modal-viewport" class="dag-modal-viewport">
<div id="dag-modal-stage" class="dag-modal-stage"></div>
</div>
</div>

<script>
(function () {
  const btn = document.getElementById("dag-zoom-btn");
  const modal = document.getElementById("dag-modal");
  const viewport = document.getElementById("dag-modal-viewport");
  const stage = document.getElementById("dag-modal-stage");
  const zoomInBtn = document.getElementById("dag-zoom-in");
  const zoomOutBtn = document.getElementById("dag-zoom-out");
  const fitBtn = document.getElementById("dag-zoom-fit");
  const closeBtn = document.getElementById("dag-modal-close");

  const MIN_SCALE = 0.2;
  const MAX_SCALE = 8;

  let scale = 1;
  let tx = 0;
  let ty = 0;
  let svgWidth = 0;
  let svgHeight = 0;
  let dragging = false;
  let dragStartX = 0;
  let dragStartY = 0;
  let dragOrigTx = 0;
  let dragOrigTy = 0;

  function clamp(value, lo, hi) {
    return Math.min(hi, Math.max(lo, value));
  }

  function applyTransform() {
    stage.style.transform =
      "translate(" + tx + "px, " + ty + "px) scale(" + scale + ")";
  }

  function fitToScreen() {
    const rect = viewport.getBoundingClientRect();
    if (!svgWidth || !svgHeight) return;
    const fitScale = Math.min(
      (rect.width - 40) / svgWidth,
      (rect.height - 40) / svgHeight,
      1,
    );
    scale = clamp(fitScale, MIN_SCALE, MAX_SCALE);
    tx = (rect.width - svgWidth * scale) / 2;
    ty = (rect.height - svgHeight * scale) / 2;
    applyTransform();
  }

  function zoomBy(factor, center) {
    const rect = viewport.getBoundingClientRect();
    const cx = center ? center.x : rect.width / 2;
    const cy = center ? center.y : rect.height / 2;
    const newScale = clamp(scale * factor, MIN_SCALE, MAX_SCALE);
    tx = cx - (cx - tx) * (newScale / scale);
    ty = cy - (cy - ty) * (newScale / scale);
    scale = newScale;
    applyTransform();
  }

  function openModal() {
    const original = document.querySelector("#dag-figure svg");
    if (!original) return;
    const clone = original.cloneNode(true);
    const viewBox = clone.getAttribute("viewBox");
    if (viewBox) {
      const parts = viewBox.trim().split(/\s+/).map(Number);
      if (parts.length === 4) {
        svgWidth = parts[2];
        svgHeight = parts[3];
        clone.setAttribute("width", svgWidth);
        clone.setAttribute("height", svgHeight);
      }
    }
    clone.removeAttribute("style");
    stage.innerHTML = "";
    stage.appendChild(clone);
    modal.classList.add("open");
    modal.setAttribute("aria-hidden", "false");
    document.body.style.overflow = "hidden";
    fitToScreen();
  }

  function closeModal() {
    modal.classList.remove("open");
    modal.setAttribute("aria-hidden", "true");
    document.body.style.overflow = "";
    stage.innerHTML = "";
  }

  btn.addEventListener("click", openModal);
  closeBtn.addEventListener("click", closeModal);
  zoomInBtn.addEventListener("click", function () {
    zoomBy(1.25);
  });
  zoomOutBtn.addEventListener("click", function () {
    zoomBy(1 / 1.25);
  });
  fitBtn.addEventListener("click", fitToScreen);

  document.addEventListener("keydown", function (event) {
    if (event.key === "Escape" && modal.classList.contains("open")) {
      closeModal();
    }
  });

  viewport.addEventListener(
    "wheel",
    function (event) {
      if (!modal.classList.contains("open")) return;
      event.preventDefault();
      const rect = viewport.getBoundingClientRect();
      const center = {
        x: event.clientX - rect.left,
        y: event.clientY - rect.top,
      };
      zoomBy(event.deltaY < 0 ? 1.1 : 1 / 1.1, center);
    },
    { passive: false },
  );

  viewport.addEventListener("mousedown", function (event) {
    dragging = true;
    dragStartX = event.clientX;
    dragStartY = event.clientY;
    dragOrigTx = tx;
    dragOrigTy = ty;
    viewport.classList.add("dragging");
  });

  window.addEventListener("mousemove", function (event) {
    if (!dragging) return;
    tx = dragOrigTx + (event.clientX - dragStartX);
    ty = dragOrigTy + (event.clientY - dragStartY);
    applyTransform();
  });

  window.addEventListener("mouseup", function () {
    dragging = false;
    viewport.classList.remove("dragging");
  });
})();
</script>

## Reading the graph

- **Macro (top)**: written down in advance, on a fixed calendar, and
  never a function of anything the simulated people do. This is
  [Theory](theory.qmd)'s Definition 1, and it is what licenses the
  counterfactual-replay result in Section 8: editing only this layer and
  replaying the rest with random draws held fixed gives an exact causal
  effect, not an estimate.
- **The opening decision**: a one-shot mixed integer program, solved once
  against a noisy belief about true demand the owner never observes
  directly (Section 3).
- **Micro (middle)**: recomputed fresh every period from the current
  state, never scripted. The purchase choice feeds the customer's own
  pantry state one period later, the one real feedback loop inside the
  micro layer.
- **True state**: always exactly conserved. Every unit bought, sold, or
  spoiled is accounted for, and every euro that moves is tracked, before
  any paperwork noise is applied.
- **Recording (bottom)**: a one-way tap. Defects are drawn from the true
  state and written into `visible/`, but never fed back into the
  simulation. This is why `hidden/` can grade `visible/` (see [Checking
  ground truth](ground-truth.qmd)) without the grading process itself
  disturbing the thing being graded.

## Read more

- [Theory](theory.qmd): the full model specification each node in this
  graph implements.
- [Checking ground truth](ground-truth.qmd): every `hidden_*` table this
  graph's recording layer feeds, and a worked example of grading against
  one.
- [Analysis catalog](analysis-catalog.qmd): the 62 questions that walk
  this graph in the other direction, from `visible/` data back toward a
  cause.
